PS-CAC-001.author: m.berger
PS-CAC-001.text: The four closure reason codes are documented as reporting-only. But a BANK-RISK closure means the bank's own risk review already flagged this client — shouldn't that code at least drive priority handling or a tighter SLA? Please confirm whether the reason code is genuinely inert downstream, or whether BANK-RISK should change the handling.

PS-CAC-001.author: Process Specialist
PS-CAC-001.text:
Incorporated: BANK-RISK cases are now documented as priority-queued with a 5-business-day cycle-time target (vs. the standard 10-business-day target for CLIENT, BANK-COMMERCIAL and DORMANCY). The control path at steps 3–4 remains identical for all codes — only queuing priority and cycle-time target differ. "What happens" block updated accordingly (provenance reset to proposed; re-confirm on approval).

Note for follow-up: M-CAC-003 (Standard closure cycle time, currently "Within 10 business days") does not reflect the BANK-RISK differentiation. A dedicated BANK-RISK cycle-time metric element, or a split of M-CAC-003 by code, may be warranted to make the target measurable.
